title: Stormtrooper X

{{Quote|Known only as Stormtrooper X, this target is not to be taken lightly. He's made a name for himself as a pirate working in the Outer Rim.|A bounty posting on Stormtrooper X, located within the Imperial Enforcement DataCore|<ref name="Gamer" />}}
'''Stormtrooper X''' was the alias of a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] who ran a [[Stormtrooper X's pirate gang|pirate gang]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. Donning a [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] helmet with a [[red]] "X" painted over it, Stormtrooper X was wanted by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] and his identity was almost universally unknown, though many of his fellow criminals considered him to be dangerous. He was once hired as a [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] and mercenary by the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] [[Narbuj Desilijic Preor]]. However, Stormtrooper X and his gang later raided one of Preor's safehouses, prompting the Hutt to post a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] of 20,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] on him. This posting was eventually archived in the [[Imperial Enforcement DataCore/Legends|Imperial Enforcement DataCore]].

==Biography==
{{Quote|The sponsor, a Hutt crimelord named Narbuj Desilijic Preor, claims X's pirate group raided one of his safehouses and made off with a Hutt's mass in spice and credits.|A bounty posting on Stormtrooper X, located within the Imperial Enforcement DataCore|<ref name="Gamer" />}}
Stormtrooper X was an alias used by a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] in the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] whose true identity was unknown to most. He made a name for himself by working as a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] and was eventually able to build up a [[Stormtrooper X's pirate gang|gang]] of at least a dozen other outlaws. He had dealings with several different underworld organizations and was wanted by the Empire, although Imperial inquiries into the mercenary ultimately yielded no results. He was known to have flown an [[N-1 starfighter/Legends|N-1 starfighter]] with the [[Transponder code/Legends|transponder code]] designation ''[[Rogue Imperial 5]]''.<ref name="Gamer" />

Stormtrooper X was contracted as a mercenary and [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] by a [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]] named [[Narbuj Desilijic Preor]], though he later ended up betraying Preor by breaking into one of the Hutt's safehouses with his pirate gang and running off with a substantial amount of the Hutt's stores of [[Spice/Legends|spice]] and [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]]. Preor placed a [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] of 20,000 credits on Stormtrooper X, wanted [[Death/Legends|dead]] or alive. This bounty posting was later archived in the [[Imperial Enforcement DataCore/Legends|Imperial Enforcement DataCore]].<ref name="Gamer" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|Stormtrooper X leads at least a dozen other outlaws, and all are considered very dangerous.|A bounty posting on Stormtrooper X, located within the Imperial Enforcement DataCore|<ref name="Gamer" />}}
Stormtrooper X and his pirate group were considered very dangerous by the denizens of the criminal underworld. He was a Human male, although his past remained a mystery to most. The Empire thought him important enough to issue a warrant for his arrest, though they offered no reward for his capture. He could speak several different languages, including [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Basic]], [[Dosh/Legends|Dosh]], [[Huttese/Legends|Huttese]], [[Rodese/Legends|Rodese]], and [[Sullustese/Legends|Sullustese]].<ref name="Gamer" />

==Equipment==
Stormtrooper X [[Clothing/Legends|wore]] a modified [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] [[Helmet/Legends|helmet]] with a large [[red]] "X" painted across the faceplate. The helmet contained a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] with a [[Broadband antenna/Legends|broadband antenna]] that increased its range to 400 [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]], as well as a [[signal interceptor]] that allowed Stormtrooper X to eavesdrop on encrypted communications. He also wore a red and brown [[Jacket/Legends|jacket]] and a [[Belt/Legends|belt]] to which a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] was attached.<ref name="Gamer" />

==Behind the scenes==
Stormtrooper X was first referenced in [[Star Wars Gamer 6|''Star Wars Gamer'' 6]] article "[[Bounties to Die For]]", written by [[S. Mitch Ritchie]] and illustrated by [[Ramón Pérez]]. The article was presented as an [[in-universe]] database of bounties posted throughout the Empire<ref name="Gamer" /> and was published on [[August 14]], [[2001]].<ref name="SW.com">{{SWArchive|url=gaming/other/rpg/news20010815.html|text=Hunting Down ''Gamer'' #6}}</ref>

The cover of the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] [[Boba Fett ½|''Boba Fett'' ½]], illustrated by [[Timothy Bradstreet]] and published in [[December]] [[1997]], features an individual resembling Stormtrooper X in a law enforcement lineup. Like Stormtrooper X, this individual wore a stormtrooper helmet, albeit without a red "X" painted on it, and with normal clothing worn underneath. The back of the comic features a sketch of this cover and describes the individual as "some mysterious fellow with an appreciation for used stormtrooper armor."<ref name="Salvage">[[Boba Fett ½|''Boba Fett'' ½]]</ref>
